steps.title: Read the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: The line crosses the $y$-axis at $(0, -1)$, so the constant term is $b = -1$.

steps.title: Compute the slope from two clear points
steps.content:
The line also passes through $(1, -6)$. Slope is:
$$m = \frac{-6 - (-1)}{1 - 0} = \frac{-5}{1} = -5$$

steps.title: Assemble the equation and test each choice
steps.content:
With $m = -5$ and $b = -1$: $f(x) = -5x - 1$.
  • Choice A ($-14x - 9$): slope and intercept both wrong.
  • Choice B ($-5x - 1$): matches both slope and intercept.
  • Choice C ($-5x + 8$): correct slope but intercept $+8$ doesn't match $-1$.
  • Choice D ($-14x + 8$): wrong slope and intercept.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
